records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dispossessed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Dispossessing.]

defs: To put out of possession; to deprive of the actual occupancy of, particularly of land or real estate; to disseize; to eject; -- usually followed by of before the thing taken away; as, to dispossess a king of his crown.
quotes: Usurp the land, and dispossess the swain.
